records.ety: [OE. hente, henten, fr. AS. hentan, gehentan, to pursue, take, seize; cf. Icel. henda, Goth. hinpan (in compos.), and E. hunt.]

defs: To seize; to lay hold on; to catch; to get.
quotes: This cursed Jew him hente and held him fast.
But all that he might of his friendes hente On bookes and on learning he it spente.
